text.title: event-sourcing-implementation Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the event-sourcing-implementation methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Append with expected_version; reject if the stream moved. Never overwrite an existing version row.
rule.rationale: Optimistic concurrency on (aggregate_id, version) UNIQUE constraint is the only safe append model.

rule.statement: Events table is append-only at the grant level: REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE FROM PUBLIC. Database enforces immutability.
rule.rationale: Application-level append-only is bypassable; grant-level is not.

rule.statement: Snapshot when replay length exceeds threshold (default N=50 events). Snapshots are additive — never overwrite an existing snapshot row.
rule.rationale: Snapshots bound replay cost; their immutability preserves audit trail.

rule.statement: Projection handlers consume events through the EventBus AFTER successful append. Failed projection retries via a poison-letter queue; never block append.
rule.rationale: Coupled projection + append produces dual-write inconsistency.

rule.statement: Integration tests cover replay determinism: given a fixed event stream, two replays produce byte-identical state.
rule.rationale: Non-deterministic replay invalidates the audit-trail promise.

rule.statement: Skip when the input does not satisfy the Applies-If preconditions or another methodology owns this output shape.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work and wrong-tool application.
